vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

verify.rule: Each interface must have a reciprocal connection_type + DOF + clearance_mm + required_feature.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to avionics heat dissipation.
risk.mit: Implement active cooling system in avionics bay.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure due to wing spar load.
risk.mit: Conduct load tests and reinforce critical joints.

risk.desc: Limited accessibility for avionics maintenance.
risk.mit: Design larger service panels and clear access paths.

risk.desc: Possible water ingress through camera window.
risk.mit: Use waterproof seals and gaskets around camera window.

risk.desc: Risk of vibration-induced loosening of fasteners.
risk.mit: Apply vibration-resistant fasteners and locking compounds.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.description: Nacelle loads carried into fuselage spine via spar_socket
Heat from avionics dissipated through shell
Structural load from wing spar to landing gear mount

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: after wing ribs, before avionics bay
after avionics tray, before GPS boom
after avionics bay, before belly camera

manufacturing_strategy.support: core support for FDM, tooling pins for CNC

verify.value: All required features are present and properly named.

cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value.

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to varying material properties.
risk.mit: Use materials with similar thermal expansion coefficients.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient fastener preload leading to joint loosening.
risk.mit: Ensure proper torque and use locking washers.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure under extreme load conditions.
risk.mit: Conduct additional load testing and review structural analysis.

risk.desc: Potential for water ingress due to improper sealing.
risk.mit: Use high-quality seals and conduct water resistance testing.

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate service access for maintenance.
risk.mit: Design additional access panels and ensure clear service paths.

verify.value: All required fields are present.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on the motor and ESC due to high ambient temperatures.
risk.mit: Implement a more robust cooling system.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to unclear assembly sequence.
risk.mit: Clarify the assembly sequence in the cad_brief.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

physics_paths.path: Rotor thrust 20-200N, tilt servo 5-30Nm, 3g maneuvering load from rotor to wing structure via bearing seats
Cooling air flow from rotor to nacelle cooling duct
Heat from motor and ESC dissipated through cooling duct to ambient air
Servo-driven worm gear rotating nacelle along tilt axis
Electrical harness from wing spar to nacelle motor and ESC
Access for motor and ESC replacement through nacelle access cover

risk.desc: Potential for heat buildup in servo mount due to insufficient cooling fins
risk.mit: Increase the number of cooling fins on the servo mount to improve heat dissipation

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to lack of detailed alignment guides
risk.mit: Add alignment pins and guides to the tilt axis and bearing seat for precise assembly

risk.desc: Possibility of electrical interference between harness passthrough and other subsystems
risk.mit: Ensure proper shielding and spacing of electrical harnesses to minimize interference

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate preload on critical joints leading to premature failure
risk.mit: Verify preload and torque settings during assembly to ensure proper preload on critical joints

risk.desc: Potential for water ingress through service access points
risk.mit: Seal service access points with appropriate gaskets and O-rings to prevent water ingress

Heat from servo to ambient via fins
Power and signal from control board to servo

manufacturing_strategy.implications: Support structures and orientation affect print quality and post-processing

risk.desc: Potential interference between harness_channel and service_window due to tight spacing.
risk.mit: Review and adjust spacing between harness_channel and service_window to ensure clearance.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion affecting harness_channel due to high thermal load.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management strategies such as heat sinks or insulation around the harness_channel.

risk.desc: Service hatch may be difficult to access in certain orientations.
risk.mit: Design service hatch to be easily accessible from multiple angles or include a tool for better access.

risk.desc: Potential for harness strain relief to fail under repeated motion.
risk.mit: Increase strain relief features and ensure they are robust enough to withstand repeated motion.

risk.desc: Risk of mis-mating connectors due to lack of keying or labeling.
risk.mit: Ensure all connectors are keyed or labeled to prevent mis-mating.
